name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Alignment rounds down, so the result must never exceed the input

name: align_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_always_zero(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == 0usize,
{
}
reason: A trivially-zero alignment function would satisfy divisibility but discard address information

name: align_identity_when_unaligned
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_identity_when_unaligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 1,
        a as nat % b as nat != 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == a,
{
}
reason: When the input is not aligned, the result must differ from the input; identity here means no rounding occurred

name: aligned_implies_zero
code:
proof fn phi_4_aligned_implies_zero(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        addr == 0nat,
{
}
reason: Many non-zero addresses are aligned; if only zero satisfies alignment the spec is degenerate

name: aligned_false_compose
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_false_compose(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        b > 1,
        c > 1,
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(a, c),
    ensures
        aligned(a, b * c),
{
}
reason: Alignment to b and c individually does not imply alignment to b*c (e.g. 12 is aligned to 4 and 6 but not 24)
